title: How 2026's Geopolitical Crises Are Reshaping the Aluminum Market
summary: Geopolitical crises, including potential US-Iran conflicts, are predicted to drive LME aluminum prices to $3,500-$4,200 per ton due to supply disruptions and panic buying. Long-term supply chain restructuring and high energy costs could stabilize prices above $3,000 per ton, with domestic prices ranging 20,500-25,000 yuan per ton. The market may show a shallow 'M' shape trend throughout 2026.

title: After US tariffs, aluminium price surge due to Middle East conflict causing concern to Coca-Cola
summary: Aluminium prices have surged to a 4-year high, approaching $3,500 per tonne in March 2026, driven by Middle East conflicts. US tariffs are exacerbating the price increases, raising concerns for industries like Coca-Cola that rely on aluminum.

title: Aluminum Prices Rise Amid Iran War
summary: Aluminum prices have reached $3,370 per ton on the London Stock Exchange, a four-year high, amid escalating tensions involving Iran. Aluminum Bahrain, a major producer, faces disruptions contributing to the surge.

title: How Trump's steel and aluminum tariffs impact prices one year later
summary: One year after Trump's steel and aluminum tariffs, domestic steel shipments are up nearly 5%, but consumer prices for steel mill products have risen over 20% and aluminum nearly 40%. The tariffs remain intact despite some being struck down, leading to mixed industry results and higher costs.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast, pushing it above $4000/t
summary: ING Group forecasts aluminum prices to exceed $4,000 per tonne due to the Strait of Hormuz closure amid escalating Middle East geopolitical conflicts. This revision reflects severe supply disruptions in a key aluminum production route.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs amid Middle East shipping disruptions. China's primary aluminum output in January and February 2026 rose 3% year-over-year to 7.53 million metric tonnes, driven by improved profit margins of 7,879 Yuan per ton. This increase reflects higher profitability in construction, packaging, and auto sectors despite broader industrial overcapacity concerns.

title: Aluminum Rises to 3½-Year Highs
summary: Aluminum futures reached $2,970 per tonne, the highest since May 2022, following South32's announcement to place its Mozal smelter in Mozambique under care and maintenance by March 2026 due to power issues. This shutdown, combined with disruptions at Iceland’s Grundartangi smelter and declining Japanese inventories, tightens global supplies. China nears its 45-million-ton annual output cap, supporting prices up nearly 17% this year.

title: Aluminum Prices on a Sustained Rise – Industry Trends and Best Practices
summary: China's electrolytic aluminum capacity is capped at 45 million tons, with 44.43 million tons operational by end-2025 and no net additions expected in 2026. Overseas growth is limited by high electricity costs in Europe/North America and infrastructure issues elsewhere. Middle East tensions, accounting for 9% of global output, have caused production curtailments and shipping delays via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Aluminum Prices Surge as Strait of Hormuz Disruptions Threaten Global Supply
summary: Aluminum prices surged to $3,340 per tonne, highest since April 2022, after a Middle East producer halted shipments due to Strait of Hormuz tensions. Qatar reduced production, UAE manages inventories, and Goldman Sachs warns of $3,600 if disruptions persist. This compounds supply risks from the upcoming Mozal smelter shutdown in Mozambique.

title: How 2026's Geopolitical Crises Are Reshaping the Aluminum Market
summary: Global aluminum market faces a slight 250,000-ton surplus in 2026 but deficits in 2027-2028 amid geopolitical turmoil. Gulf countries (UAE, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia) produced 6.87 million tons in 2024, vulnerable to Iran conflict spillover affecting power and transport. Long-term, supply chains may shift to Africa/South America, pushing prices above $3,000 per ton.

title: After US tariffs, aluminium price surge due to Middle East conflict causing concern to Coca-Cola
summary: Aluminum prices surged to a 4-year high near $3,500 per tonne in March 2026, driven by Middle East conflict and US tariffs. This price rise impacts downstream users like Coca-Cola. Combined factors exacerbate supply concerns in a tight market.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast pushing it above 4000 t
summary: ING Group forecasts aluminum prices above $4,000 per tonne due to escalating Middle East geopolitical conflict and potential Strait of Hormuz closure. This revision highlights severe supply risks from the region. Prices reflect broader market tightening.

title: The Future Of Aluminum: Trends And Innovations in 2026
summary: The article highlights high-end alloy innovations, green transformation through circular economy, and intelligent manufacturing in aluminum for 2026. It details Zhonglian's efforts in low-carbon production, including 30% clean energy adoption reducing carbon footprint by 22% per ton, direct aluminum liquid supply cutting energy by 15%, and 95% scrap recycling. Modular profiles and eco-friendly finishes support 100% recyclability and reduced waste.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The global aluminum scrap market is surging due to policy shifts, high prices, and demand for sustainability, with Asia-Pacific leading via closed-loop recycling and green smelting. Recycled aluminum uses up to 95% less energy than primary production, aiding climate goals, especially in construction, automotive, and electronics sectors. Buyers are advised to diversify sources and invest in traceability amid volatility.

title: Next Phase of Aluminium Industry by Dr. Anupam Agnihotri
summary: Dr. Agnihotri discusses technology, transition, and transformation in the aluminum industry, emphasizing power decarbonization as key to reducing carbon footprint linked to electricity mix. Incremental improvements like smelter efficiency, renewable integration, and scaling secondary recycling (using 95% less energy) are highlighted as cost-effective paths. Inert anode technologies are expected by 2030 for further decarbonization.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,148 CNY/T on March 20, 2026, up 0.48% from the previous day, with a 3.79% increase over the past month despite being 0.25% lower year-over-year. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,140 per ton to six-week highs following China's pledge to curb excess capacity. Futures held steady above CNY 3,070 per ton near one-month highs amid efforts to strengthen the industry amid oversupply and export constraints.

title: US Steel Production Up 4.9% Year-to-Date Through March 14, 2026
summary: U.S. raw steel production for the week ending March 14, 2026, was 1.774 million net tons at a 76.7% utilization rate, up 4.2% from the same week in 2025 but down 0.9% from the prior week. Year-to-date production reached 18.613 million net tons at 77.2% utilization, a 4.9% increase over the same period last year. The report also covers price analysis, trade dynamics, export/import trends, and regional production contributions.

title: Stocks of main finished steel products in China up 7.0% in early March 2026
summary: Domestic inventories of five main finished steel products in 21 major Chinese cities totaled 11.64 million mt as of March 10, 2026, up 7.0% or 0.76 million mt from February 28. Inventories of HRC, CRC, wire rod, and rebar rose by 2.5%, 4.1%, 6.4%, and 12.9% respectively, while medium steel plate inventories fell 1.5%. This reflects ongoing market trends amid recent price movements in China.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues to climb above $1,000 per ton. Factors include continued trade restrictions limiting access to North American supply outside the United States and ongoing market dynamics.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: Domestic raw steel production increased 6.2% year-over-year in the week ending February 28, with year-to-date production up 5% and capability utilization rate rising. Steel pipe prices rose 7-10% nationally due to demand from data centers and spring construction. The update also covers Europe's steel industry push for local prioritization and a new U.S. steel facility investment.

title: Viewpoint: US steel tariffs cause scrap supply glut
summary: US steel tariffs are creating a surplus of ferrous scrap supply, likely persisting into 2026, dampening prices outside the US due to Chinese production. US flat and long steel prices are climbing while Turkish rebar prices decline, encouraging domestic sales. #1 busheling scrap averaged $390/gt, up 4%, amid rising hot-rolled coil prices.

title: Steel News
summary: Tata Steel signed an MoU with University of Science and Technology Beijing for collaboration. HBIS Tangsteel started its fourth continuous slab caster from Primetals Technologies. Other recent updates include Algoma Steel's new EAF running 24/7 and various EAF upgrades and certifications boosting production capacity.

title: Steel Dynamics Looks to Strong Demand for Aluminum, Steel Operations
summary: Steel Dynamics anticipates strong demand and stable pricing for flat-rolled steel in 2026, supported by lower imports and improved operations at its Sinton, Texas mill. North American automotive production is expected to match 2025 levels. The company reports record shipments and biocarbon production refinements.

title: Steel Consuming Market Outlook March 2026
summary: Construction planning remains high despite cooling, supporting future steel demand. Residential housing starts rose 7.2% in January 2026, driven by steel-intensive multi-family projects. HVAC shipments face short-term pressure but long-term fundamentals are solid.

title: The Manufacturer Daily News Digest - 20/03/2026
summary: UK steel strategy aims to boost domestic production to half of consumption needs, with reduced import quotas and 50% tariffs on excess imports. Published yesterday, it targets higher output and import cuts. Other stories cover CBAM impacts on cement and UK industrial competitiveness.

title: Future of Steel: Trends, Technology & Sustainability 2026
summary: The article explores upcoming steel trends focusing on sustainability, including direct reduced iron (DRI) technology, hydrogen-based operations, and steel scrap recycling to create a circular economy with minimal waste. It highlights technologies like molten oxide electrolysis and scrap-based manufacturing for low-carbon steel production. By 2026, advancements in scrap sorting with AI and chemical analysis are expected to enhance reliability and efficiency.

title: A streamlined way to make steel could reduce America's reliance on imports
summary: Hertha Metals is scaling a single-step steelmaking process using natural gas or hydrogen to reduce iron ore in a continuous electric arc furnace, eliminating coal, coking, and sintering. This innovation cuts energy use by 30%, reduces costs by 25%, and lowers emissions while using any grade of iron ore. The company operates a pilot plant near Houston and plans a 10,000-ton plant by late 2027.

title: LeadIT says 2026 a 'critical year' for green steel despite slowing momentum
summary: The Leadership Group for Industry Transition (LeadIT) identifies 2026 as a critical year for green steel development, despite only two green steel projects announced in 2025, indicating slowing momentum. The report emphasizes the need for accelerated progress in sustainable steel production amid global climate goals.

title: Copper Drops to 3-Month Low
summary: Copper fell to 5.34 USD/Lbs on March 20, 2026, down 1.67% from the previous day, marking a three-month low amid a strong dollar and concerns over high energy prices denting manufacturing demand. Over the past month, prices have declined 7.58%. Copper futures tracked declines in key industrial metals.

title: Copper Heads for Third Weekly Drop
summary: Copper futures rebounded to around $5.5 per pound on Friday after hitting multi-month lows, supported by reassurances from the US and Israel on the Middle East conflict. US President Donald Trump stated no ground troops against Iran, while Israeli PM Netanyahu confirmed no further strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Prices are heading for a third weekly drop.

title: China Cu prices fall on higher stocks, stronger dollar
summary: Copper prices in China retreated in March due to rising inventories and a firmer US dollar, with the SHFE April contract falling to a three-month low of 95,400 yuan/t on March 18. Combined LME and SHFE inventories rose 56% to 745,283t by March 13, reflecting higher refined output and slower demand recovery post-Lunar New Year. LME three-month copper also declined over the period.

title: Bare Bright Copper Scrap Prices – as of March 21, 2026
summary: Bare bright copper scrap prices reported as of March 21, 2026, with national average around $3.66/lb and local yard prices ranging from $4.20/lb to $6.00/lb in recent days. Users can track daily national scrap prices and 30-day history via the iScrap App. Prices reflect market trends in scrap metal recycling.

title: Price LME Copper March 2026 forward (trade count)
summary: LME Copper March 2026 forward showed bid at $13,009, ask at $13,027.50, and trade at $13,041 with cumulative volume of 165 and trade size of 1. This reflects latest forward market trading activity for copper. Data provides real-time insights into LME copper futures.

title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
summary: Global copper faces a structural supply deficit driving prices to $12,000-$14,500 per ton, exacerbated by China's top smelters cutting production by over 10% in 2026 due to insufficient ore concentrate. This crisis delays renewable projects like offshore wind farms and raises EV prices, fueling 'greenflation.' Focus remains on restarts at Cobre Panama and Grasberg mines to avert further shortages.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: US copper prices are set for volatility in 2026 amid uncertain Chinese demand and supply deficits from delayed new mines and slipping South American output. Market participants anticipate additional foreign production to meet demand despite high US inventories. Forecasts vary, with Citigroup predicting over $5.90/lb by Q2 2026.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: The Copper for Tomorrow bid seeks to address challenges in the copper value chain to boost sustainable production amid rising demand for clean energy transition. It tackles the paradox of increasing output from lower-grade ores using less energy, water, and waste, while electrifying mining operations. The initiative unites industry and researchers to meet ESG goals and double global production sustainably over the next 30 years.

title: Copper famine: A case for downstream leadership
summary: Downstream companies can mitigate copper shortages through investments in recycling, secondary smelting, and innovations like modular smelters, AI sorting, and advanced electrolytic processes. Recycled copper scrap is projected to grow 4% annually to 14 million tons by 2050, helping bridge the supply-demand gap for energy transition. Strategic partnerships and vertical integration align carbon goals with production growth.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 1.08% to $16,853.62 per ton, with Chinese markets at ¥116,056 per ton. This pullback reflects risk-off sentiment from Middle East tensions and a stronger US dollar, alongside global oversupply. Downside is capped by Indonesia’s reduced ore quotas and steady Chinese stainless steel demand.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel fell to 16,885 USD/T on March 20, 2026, down 1.05% from the previous day and 2.31% over the past month. Prices slid amid rising global risk aversion from Middle East tensions, higher oil prices, inflation concerns, and a stronger US dollar pressuring industrial metals. It remains 4.16% higher year-over-year.

title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers
summary: On March 20, 2026, nickel prices show a cautious tone with the latest quote at $17,299/tonne on March 18, up 0.25%, remaining flat monthly but 5.39% higher yearly. Support comes from Indonesia’s output cuts rather than demand rebound in stainless steel or batteries. Copper faces more pressure from inventories, while nickel is policy-sensitive.

title: Asia's EVolution: No clear skies in Indonesia's nickel mining towns
summary: Coverage of Indonesia's nickel mining sector and its environmental and operational challenges in mining towns.

title: Nickel Futures Rebound
summary: Nickel futures declined to around $17,100 per tonne in mid-March amid global risk aversion, reflecting market volatility as Indonesia's production cuts take effect.

title: Nickel Mining Industry Report 2026: Production by Country, Prices, Companies, Major Operating Mines, Competitive Landscape, Major Exploration and Development Projects 2025-2035
summary: Comprehensive industry report indicating global nickel production increased 6.1% to 4.0Mt in 2025, with Indonesia leading growth. Production is expected to increase 9.4% to 4.4Mt in 2026, though Indonesia's announced cuts may alter this trajectory.

title: NOVONIX’s Patented All-Dry, Zero-Waste Cathode Synthesis Technology
summary: Dr. Mark McArthur from NOVONIX discusses their patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology for nickel-based cathodes. This innovation is expected to significantly reduce costs and environmental impact in battery production. It aligns with sustainability goals in nickel technology for electric vehicles.

title: Nickel-Containing Stainless Steel for Green Hydrogen Production
summary: Green hydrogen offers unlimited potential as a fossil fuel replacement for net-zero goals. Nickel-containing stainless steel plays a key sustainable role in this transition. The article highlights its importance in climate-neutral future technologies.

title: Demand for Minerals to Power Technology Could Triple by 2030, UN Political Chief Warns
summary: UN Undersecretary-General Rosemary DiCarlo states that demand for critical minerals like nickel, powering tech from smartphones to energy transition, could triple by 2030 and quadruple by 2040. Trade in these minerals reached $2.5 trillion in 2023, driving the 21st-century economy. International cooperation is urged to address supply imbalances amid geopolitical tensions.

title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
summary: Tenaris will showcase its portfolio of hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy, from March 24-27. The company highlighted recent operational upgrades at its Piombino facility in Italy, including expanded product range and enhanced production capabilities. The pipes have been certified with Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) assessing their lifecycle environmental impact.

title: Tenaris to supply line pipe, OCTG, coating and services for Sakarya Phase 3 in offshore Turkey
summary: Tenaris has been awarded a contract to supply seamless and welded pipes for the third phase of the Sakarya gas field project, with production occurring at its Dalmine mill in Italy and Confab mill in Brazil. The contract includes 22,700 tons of seamless pipes and 87,800 tons of welded pipes with anticorrosion coating applied at Tenaris facilities. The project demonstrates Tenaris's integrated One Line® service model combining pipeline solutions with coating services.

title: Tenaris supports HRSG projects worldwide to meet rising electricity demand
summary: Tenaris secured agreements for 15 heat recovery steam generator (HRSG) projects in 2025, totaling over 17,000 tons of pipes produced at its Silcotub mill in Romania. These projects support combined-cycle power generation by capturing waste heat from gas turbines to improve plant efficiency. The company emphasized its integrated supply chain capabilities in managing complex projects with demanding technical specifications and tight schedules.
